name: Mediterranean Blue Mussels & Cilantro - Scully on Spring
recipeIngredient: 2 Red cherry peppers; thinly sliced, up to 3 peppers
2 tbsp Coriander seeds; toasted and crushed
1 small bunch Cilantro; roughly chopped, leaf only
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash mussels under cold running water to remove any beard or growth on the shell. Reserve in the refrigerator.
2. Slice cherry peppers thin, removing seeds for a more mild spice.
3. Chop shallots and garlic fine.
4. Toast coriander seeds until their fragrance is released, then crush the seeds with the blade of a knife.
5. Slice scallions thin on a bias.
6. Chop roughly the leaves of cilantro that has been washed and dried well.
7. Heat a small stockpot or large saute pan over medium flame. Add extra virgin olive oil, garlic, shallots, coriander seeds, and cherry peppers. Cook for about 2 minutes to release their flavors; do not brown them.
8. Add mussels and toss to coat with all onions and spices. Then add white wine, scallions, and cilantro. Toss again and cover the pan. Cook until mussels open; at this point they are done.
9. Butter can be added during the last minute of cooking if desired.
10. Serve mussels immediately. Crisp grilled bread can be served on the side to soak up the cooking broth that remains in the pan.
Yield: Serves 4 as an appetizer
